steps.instruction: This is a bead-based purification of nucleic acids using either BioRad 96 well plates or Grenier 96 well plates. Using P300 single and multi channel pipettes we have flexibility using 4-in-1 tube racks or 12 well reservoirs to hold beads. The beads can be diluted with 40% PEG 8000 mixtures or left neat from the reagent bottle. Explanation of complex parameters below: * `Number of samples`: Specify the number of samples this run (1-96) * `Reaction Volume`: Specify the starting sample volume in uL for the first plate * `Bead Ratio`: Specify the ratio of beads to sample volume with a float. Default is 1.8 * `Elution Volume`: Specify the volume of elution liquid to use and subsequently transfer to the final plate in uL * `P300 Multi Channel Pipette Mount`: Specify which side the multi-channel P300 is mounted to. The single-channel P300 will default to the opposite side * `Well Plate Type`: Change between the BioRad plate in the OT-2 default labware library and the custom definition for Greiner 96 well plates with a circular well * `Flash`: Specify whether the robot will flash on pauses for tip rack refills and trash alerts ---
Beads are added to the sample plate according to the supplied bead ratio parameter. The sample/bead mixture is mixed. The beads are re-mixed every 3 samples to ensure homogeneity in the reagent well.
The beads are incubated for 10 minutes in the samples before the magnetic module is engaged for 7 minutes
The supernatant is removed and deposited in the waste container in slot 11
Ethanol is added to the wells to wash beads. 200 uL is added for the Greiner plates and 120 uL for BioRad plates
A 2 minute delay occurs to allow beads to re-pellet after ethanol wash
Ethanol is removed from samples and deposited in the waste container in slot 11
Steps 4-6 are repeated for a second ethanol wash
Water is added to the samples to elute according to the set parameters below and mixed
A 5 minute delay occurs to allow elution to occur followed by the magnetic module being engaged for 7 minutes
The elution solution, now separated from the beads, is aspirated and dispensed in the awaiting plate in slot 10
